'# The following code to create a dataframe and remove duplicated rows is always executed and acts as a preamble for your script: 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report

# dataset = pandas.DataFrame(actual_sentiment, predicted_sentiment, confidence_score, text)
# dataset = dataset.drop_duplicates()

# Paste or type your script code here:

y_test = dataset[''actual_sentiment'']
y_predict = dataset[''predicted_sentiment'']

report = classification_report(y_test, y_predict, output_dict=True, zero_division=0)

df_report = pd.DataFrame(report).transpose()

df_class_metrics = df_report.drop([''accuracy'', ''macro avg'', ''weighted avg''], errors=''ignore'')

plot_data = df_class_metrics[[''precision'', ''recall'', ''f1-score'']]

print("\nClass-wise Metrics:")
print(plot_data)

plt.rcParams.update({''font.size'': 12})

fig_height = max(6, len(plot_data) * 0.5)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, fig_height))

cax = ax.imshow(plot_data.values, cmap=''Blues'', vmin=0, vmax=1, aspect=''auto'')

ax.set_xticks(np.arange(len(plot_data.columns)))
ax.set_xticklabels(plot_data.columns, fontsize=14, fontweight=''bold'')
ax.xaxis.tick_top()

ax.set_yticks(np.arange(len(plot_data.index)))
ax.set_yticklabels(plot_data.index, fontsize=12)

cbar = fig.colorbar(cax, ax=ax, fraction=0.046, pad=0.04)
cbar.set_label(''Score'', rotation=270, labelpad=15)

for i in range(len(plot_data.index)):
    for j in range(len(plot_data.columns)):
        val = plot_data.values[i, j]
        text_color = "white" if val > 0.5 else "black"
        ax.text(j, i, f"{val:.1%}",
                ha="center", va="center", color=text_color, fontsize=12)

ax.set_title(''Sentiment Class-wise Performance Matrix'', pad=40, fontsize=16)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()'
